articles.title: Iran says it has closed Strait of Hormuz over Israeli attacks in Lebanon
articles.summary: The US military has disputed Tehran's claim, which comes ahead of US-Iran talks in Switzerland on Sunday.

articles.title: Lebanese turtle conservationist Mona Khalil killed by Israeli strike
articles.summary: Mona Khalil, who had refused to leave the beach she had spent years protecting, died from her injuries after the Israeli strike.

articles.title: Meloni tells Trump to 'focus on your own popularity' as row escalates
articles.summary: The US president earlier questioned Meloni's popularity after suggesting she "begged" for a photo at G7 summit

articles.title: Israeli strikes kill six people in Gaza including Al Jazeera cameraman, officials say
articles.summary: The Israeli military accused Ahmed Wishah of being a "Hamas sniper operative", without providing evidence.

articles.title: Bolivian president declares state of emergency
articles.summary: The move comes after weeks of anti-government protests that have caused a shortage of basic goods in Bolivia.

articles.title: Zelensky returns highest Polish honour after award stripped
articles.summary: Ukraine's president said his country was open to "engagement" about "difficult and painful chapters of our shared past".

articles.title: Tourist dies in Dominican Republic luxury resort fire
articles.summary: Nearly 1,700 people were evacuated from the huge blaze at Viva Wyndham Dominicus Beach in Bayahibe, a popular resort town on the Caribbean coast.

articles.title: US to stop funding HIV programmes in South Africa
articles.summary: More than eight million South Africans are living with HIV – the highest number of any country in the world.

articles.title: Australia confirms first case of H5N1 bird flu as virus reaches every continent
articles.summary: Australia was previously the only continent where the H5N1 bird flu strain had not yet been found.

articles.title: African and Caribbean nations call for formal apology for transatlantic slavery
articles.summary: The leaders have asked for apologies from the countries that benefited from the slave trade, as well as debt relief and financial compensation.
